steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence presents two linked questions: 'What are atmospheric rivers?' and 'how [blank]?' Both are direct questions, so two rules apply. First, auxiliary-subject inversion is required ('do they,' not 'they do'). Second, because this is a direct question, it must end with a question mark, not a period. The blank must satisfy both requirements.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — do they affect our weather.: has correct word order (do they) but ends with a period instead of a question mark — wrong punctuation for a direct question. ✗
  • Choice B — they do affect our weather.: wrong word order (subject before auxiliary) and ends with a period — fails both tests. ✗
  • Choice C — do they affect our weather?: correct auxiliary-subject inversion and correct question mark. ✓
  • Choice D — they do affect our weather?: has a question mark but wrong word order (subject before auxiliary) — fails the inversion test. ✗
That matches Choice C — do they affect our weather?.
